content:
You are a knowledge graph extraction agent. Read the conversation transcript below and extract entities and relationships.

## Entity Types
person, project, technology, concept, file, decision, task, problem

## Relationship Types
uses, depends_on, built_with, related_to, part_of, created, modified, solved, caused, blocked_by, decided, worked_on, learned, prefers

## Output
Read existing entities from /home/mohan/.claude/knowledge-graph/entities.jsonl first to avoid duplicates (match by name+type).
Append new entities to /home/mohan/.claude/knowledge-graph/entities.jsonl — one JSON per line:
{"name":"X","type":"technology","description":"...","properties":{},"session_id":"web-57bfd5b8-5b4e-4e32-87c7-36d70b896f35","date":"2026-03-19"}

Append new relationships to /home/mohan/.claude/knowledge-graph/relationships.jsonl — one JSON per line:
{"source":"X","target":"Y","type":"uses","context":"...","session_id":"web-57bfd5b8-5b4e-4e32-87c7-36d70b896f35","date":"2026-03-19"}

## Rules
- Skip trivial/generic entities. Focus on meaningful things.
- Keep descriptions under 100 chars.
- Do NOT modify existing lines — only append.
- If the conversation has no meaningful entities, write nothing.

## Conversation Transcript
Read the file at: /home/mohan/.claude/knowledge-graph/tmp2tgt7p_v.txt

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- session-logger:renameAI: AI-assisted session rename - generates a concise, descriptive name from conversation context.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

User: I'm tasked with transforming my organization into AI powerhouse in terms of using it to improve our productivity, and automating workflows so that our team can deliver more with reduced workforce.



As part of AI transformation, I created 4 levels to team members to transform into, in progressive manner.



Level-1 (AI Literate) - Use AI for common tasks. Goal: Improve "Personal" productivity by using GenAI for common day to day tasks

Level-2 (AI Enabled) - Use AI for domain specific tasks. Goal: Improve "Team" productivity by using GenAI for team/domain specific functions

Level-3 (AI Practitioner) - Realize AI based use-cases. Goal: Given detailed documentation of domain specific workflows/use-cases, automate them using LLM APIs and Tools

Level 4 (AI Architect) - Solve domain specific problems. Goal: Determine AI applicability to organization specific domains/functions, identify GenAI Tools/Techniques to apply to domain specific problems.



Our goal is for everyone to be at Level-3, and 15% to progress to Level-3 and 5% to progress to Level-4.



I created training curriculum by curating courses from Coursera, Udemy and DeepLearning.ai.



Below is the training Goal:

"Transform Organization into an AI-native powerhouse by equipping every sub-team with tailer capabilities -- from foundational productivity to advanced agentic development -- that accelerate deliverables and streamline overall workflows and operations."



Below is the training charter :

"This training program is chartered to design and execute a tiered, role-specific training curriculum that operationalizes Generative AI across development, testing and management functions, ensuring seamless integration of AI tools into all of our Organization's workflows".



As part of this, I have created an AI training roadmap for the organization and acquired necessary funding. There is a kick-off soon. I want you to create a power point presentation with below content
Assistant: The user wants me to create a PowerPoint presentation about their AI transformation initiative. Let me first read the PPTX skill to understand best practices, then create the presentation.

They mentioned an "attached pdf" but I don't see any PDF uploaded. Let me check for uploads and also read the skill file.
```

```

```

```

```

The user mentioned "attached pdf" but there's no uploaded file. I should note this and proceed with creating the presentation using the information they provided. I'll create compelling "Why Transform" slides based on general AI transformation arguments since the PDF isn't available.

Let me plan the presentation:

1. **Title Slide** - AI Transformation Kick-off
2. **Slide 2** - Goal & Charter Statement
3. **Slides 3-12** - Why We Need to Transform (up to 10 slides)
4. **Slides 13-17** - 4 Levels explanation (up to 5 slides)
5. **Slide 18** - Use Cases already in action


6. 
Now I'm thinking through the visual design with a tech-forward color scheme—something like a dark teal palette to give it that AI feel. For the content structure, I'll organize this into a Goal & Charter section, then move into the Why Transform section with slides covering the AI revolution, accelerating adoption rates, competitive pressures, productivity gains, the risks of waiting, current AI capabilities, and real-world industry examples.

I'll round out that section with slides on workforce evolution, the shift from manual to intelligent processes, and our specific opportunity. Then I'll dedicate a smaller section to the 4 Levels framework with overview and detail slides, progression paths, and expected outcomes. Finally, I'll cap it with a slide on use cases

User: Can you re-phrase below to make it simple :

OUR GOAL

Transform our Organization into an AI-native powerhouse by equipping every sub-team with tailored capabilities — from foundational productivity to advanced agentic development — that accelerate deliverables and streamline overall workflows and operations.
Assistant: Mohan wants a simpler version of the goal statement.Here are a couple of options:

**Option A (tightest):**
"Equip every team with practical AI skills — from everyday productivity to advanced automation — so we can deliver faster and work more efficiently."

**Option B (slightly more detail):**
"Build AI fluency across every sub-team so that each person can use AI to speed up their work, automate routine tasks, and help the organization deliver more with the talent we have."

**Option C (keeps the "from-to" structure):**
"Give every team the AI skills they need — from using AI for daily tasks to building automated workflows — so we deliver faster and operate more efficiently."

Want me to update the slide with whichever version you prefer?
User: Can you re-phrase below to make it simple :

OUR GOAL

Transform our Organization into an AI-native powerhouse by equipping every sub-team with tailored capabilities — from foundational productivity to advanced agentic development — that accelerate deliverables and streamline overall workflows and operations.


OUR CHARTER

This training program is chartered to design and execute a tiered, role-specific training curriculum that operationalizes Generative AI across development, testing, and management functions — ensuring seamless integration of AI tools into all of our Organization's workflows.
Assistant: Mohan wants simpler versions of both the Goal and Charter statements.Here are simplified versions for both:

**OUR GOAL**

*Option A:* "Equip every team with practical AI skills — from everyday productivity to advanced automation — so we deliver faster and work more efficiently."

*Option B:* "Make AI a natural part of how every team works — helping each person move from basic AI use to building real automations that speed up what we deliver."

**OUR CHARTER**

*Option A:* "Design and deliver a step-by-step AI training program — tailored for development, testing, and management roles — that makes AI tools a natural part of how we all work."

*Option B:* "Build a role-specific, tiered training program that helps every developer, tester, and manager integrate AI tools into their daily workflows."

A natural pairing would be **Goal Option A + Charter Option A** — they're concise, parallel in structure, and avoid jargon like "operationalizes" and "agentic development" while keeping the meaning intact. Want me to update the deck with those?
User: I would like to keep the original. Just provide me alternative phrase for "AI-native powerhouse ".
Assistant: Simple rephrasing request.Here are a few alternatives:

- **"AI-fluent organization"** — emphasizes skill, not hype
- **"AI-ready organization"** — practical, forward-looking
- **"AI-capable organization"** — grounded, no-nonsense
- **"organization where AI is second nature"** — conversational
- **"AI-skilled organization"** — simplest, most low-key

Given the toned-down direction you're going for, **"AI-fluent organization"** or **"AI-ready organization"** would land best — ambitious without sounding like a press release.
User: I like AI-fluent organization, Now re-phrase "Transform our Organization into an AI-native powerhouse" accordingly.
Assistant: Simple rephrasing."Transform our Organization into an AI-fluent organization"

Or if you want to avoid repeating "organization" twice in the same sentence:

**"Build an AI-fluent organization"**

This keeps it punchy and reads naturally with the rest of the sentence: *"Build an AI-fluent organization by equipping every sub-team with tailored capabilities..."*
User: What do you think about "This training program is chartered to design and execute a tiered, role-specific training curriculum that operationalizes Generative AI across all our responsible functional areas — ensuring seamless integration of AI tools into all of our Organization's workflows. "
Assistant: Mohan is asking for my opinion on a rephrased charter statement. Let me give honest, constructive feedback.That reads well. The swap from "development, testing, and management functions" to "all our responsible functional areas" is smart — it's more inclusive and avoids limiting the scope to just three teams.

Two small suggestions if you're open to tightening it:

**"operationalizes"** is a heavy word. You could swap it for "puts into practice" or "brings...into," but honestly in a charter statement the formality is appropriate — so it works as-is.

**"all...all"** — "all our responsible functional areas" and "all of our Organization's workflows" appear close together. You could drop the second one:

*"...ensuring seamless integration of AI tools into our Organization's workflows."*

So the polished version would be:

> "This training program is chartered to design and execute a tiered, role-specific training curriculum that operationalizes Generative AI across all our responsible functional areas — ensuring seamless integration of AI tools into our Organization's workflows."

Only one word removed, but it reads a touch cleaner. Either way it's solid.
